claims.claim: 100% MiniCheck (Tier 1) pass rate on all certified cells across all five models
claims.evidence: benchmark_output/summary.json: tier1_pass_rate=1.0 for toggle_switch, brusselator, selkov, repressilator, goodwin

claims.claim: Krawczyk contraction factors 0.63-0.90 across all models
claims.evidence: benchmark_output/summary.json: toggle=0.6609, brusselator=0.8015, selkov=0.8334, repressilator=0.6334, goodwin=0.9030

claims.claim: MiniCheck is 917 lines standalone checker with 5 models
claims.evidence: wc -l phase_cartographer/minicheck.py → 917 (toggle_switch, brusselator, selkov, repressilator, goodwin)

claims.claim: 81 tests pass (unit, integration, end-to-end, composition, GP, benchmarks)
Five benchmark models evaluated: toggle switch, Brusselator, Selkov, repressilator, Goodwin
claims.evidence: benchmark_output/summary.json contains results for all five models

claims.claim: Newton-guided search improves repressilator coverage from 25% to 100%
claims.evidence: Bisection-only: 25% coverage, 69.8s. Newton-guided: 100% coverage, 0.4s. Verified by running benchmarks with both approaches.

claims.claim: Newton-guided search improves Goodwin coverage from 0% to 100%
Krawczyk operator certifies equilibrium existence and uniqueness (Theorem 1)
claims.evidence: Standard result (Krawczyk 1969). Implemented in equilibrium/krawczyk.py, independently reimplemented in minicheck.py.

claims.claim: Delta-soundness bound (Theorem 2): explicit bound relating dReal delta to eigenvalue gap
claims.evidence: Proof in tool_paper.tex Section 7 and Appendix B. Implementation in smt/delta_bound.py. Unit-tested.
Proof in tool_paper.tex Section 9. Implementation in atlas/composition.py. Tested in tests/test_composition.py.

claims.claim: Formal regime inference rules derive labels from certified facts
claims.evidence: tiered/certificate.py::RegimeInferenceRules with 6 rules. RegimeDerivation proof trees in atlas/composition.py.

claims.claim: GP surrogate uses ARD Matern-5/2 kernel with calibration
claims.evidence: gp/surrogate.py: matern52_kernel_ard, optimize_ard_length_scales, calibration_error, train_from_atlas. Tested in tests/test_gp.py.
refinement/octree.py::gp_guided_refine integrates GP into refinement loop. Tested in tests/test_gp.py.

claims.claim: Anisotropic splitting guided by eigenvalue sensitivity
7 boundary cell pairs detected for Brusselator (Hopf bifurcation surface)

hallucination_audit.actual: Five models: toggle switch, Brusselator, Selkov, repressilator, Goodwin
84-100% coverage. Brusselator at 84.4% due to Hopf bifurcation gap.
All reported results use Tier 1 (MiniCheck). Tier 2/3 implemented but not exercised.
No comparisons with external tools were run
100% coverage with Newton-guided search (was 25% with bisection only)

limitations: Brusselator has 15.6% uncertified gap near Hopf bifurcation (correct conservative behavior)
Tier 2 (dReal) and Tier 3 (Z3) not exercised in benchmarks
GP surrogate not head-to-head benchmarked on identical problems
Many cells near bifurcation boundaries classified as INCONCLUSIVE (correct when eigenvalue enclosures cross zero)
